hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Reduce WebP file size while maintaining quality. Perfect for photos, web images, and digital content.

dropzone.orClick: or click to browse WebP files
dropzone.maxSize: Max 5MB per file - Up to 10 WebP images per upload

qualitySettings.title: Quality Settings
qualitySettings.description: WebP uses lossy compression. Lower quality reduces file size significantly but may affect image details. Recommended: 75-90% for most use cases.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: Your images are processed on-the-fly. Originals stay in your browser; compressed results are streamed back immediately.
privacy.p2: No permanent storage: the server keeps temporary buffers just long enough to encode and deliver the result, then memory is released.
privacy.p3: No analytics on image content; only anonymous aggregate performance counters may be collected (sizes, timings) for optimization.
privacy.p4: Close the tab any time after processing completes. While processing, avoid closing to prevent interruption.
privacy.notice: All processing local + server-side transient, no files stored.

status.settingsChangedDesc: Click Apply New Settings to re-compress your WebP images with the new quality configuration.

howTo.title: How To Compress WebP
howTo.step1Desc: Drag and drop your WebP files. Multi-photo upload is fully supported.
howTo.step2Desc: Turn down the quality slider slightly to see massive file size drops without visual artifacting.
howTo.step3Desc: Save your lightweight WebPs instantly, optimized for web and social media.

makeSmaller.title: Make Your WebP Smaller Without Losing Quality
makeSmaller.card1Desc: WebP compression removes unneeded data to reduce size without noticeable blur.
makeSmaller.card2Desc: Lean WebPs speed up rendering and improve user experience.
makeSmaller.card3Desc: Lower file sizes mean lower hosting costs and faster mobile downloads.

why.title: Why Compress WebP?
why.p1: Shrinking WebPs eliminates hidden metadata and applies lossy algorithms for much lighter files. This improves loading speed and SEO metrics.
why.tipText: 75%-85% quality usually gives excellent size reduction while looking almost identical.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.intro: WebP compression is lossy, trading some quality for smaller file size. Adjust the quality slider to see file size changes.
qualityGuide.recommendedDesc: Website photos - Social media - Best balance
qualityGuide.maxCompressionDesc: Previews only - Visible artifacts - Not recommended

items.question: How does WebP compression reduce file size?
items.answer: WebP uses lossy compression that removes less-visible image data, significantly reducing file size.

items.question: What is the best WebP quality percentage for websites?
items.answer: Most sites use 75%-85% quality for the best balance between image quality and file size.

items.question: Does smaller WebP size help SEO?
items.answer: Yes. Lighter WebP files improve loading speed and Core Web Vitals, which can help search rankings.

items.question: Why not use PNG instead of WebP?
items.answer: WebP is better for complex photos and gradients. PNG is better for transparency, logos, and UI graphics.
